releases.title: Minimal UI polish
releases.summary: A cleaner release focused on making About and What's New feel lighter and more professional.
highlights: About now shows only the important fork and upstream version details.
The fork signature line is softer and visually quieter.
What's New is now a smaller minimal dialog with shorter text.
userImpact: The update experience feels cleaner and the app explains itself without looking crowded.

releases.title: Shippable transparency release
releases.summary: This build fixes the release-blocking bug from 1.0.5 and keeps the new update transparency system intact.
highlights: Fixed the missing Utils import that broke the release build pipeline.
Stabilized GitHub Actions so master prepares tags while real APK publishing happens on release runs.
Retained the new What's New dialog, richer About details, and managed release notes.
userImpact: This is the first clean release target for the new update experience.
Users should no longer see a half-ready release line as the intended update destination.

releases.title: Transparent release intelligence
releases.summary: This build makes updates easier to trust by surfacing exactly what changed inside the app and on GitHub.
highlights: About now shows current-build highlights in addition to the fork identity, upstream base version, and upstream commit.
A one-time What's New dialog now appears after version changes so users immediately see what changed.
The update screen now shows release metadata such as publish date, asset count, and release channel.
GitHub releases can now be published with a proper notes body instead of an empty release page.
userImpact: Users can verify whether an update is fork-specific work, upstream sync work, or both.
The installed app no longer depends on memory or guesswork to explain the current build.
GitHub release pages and in-app update prompts now tell the same story.

releases.title: Reliable update detection
releases.summary: This build improved update detection when GitHub release assets are still being attached.
highlights: New versions are now surfaced even when a device-specific APK is not ready yet.
The update action can fall back to the release page instead of failing silently.
userImpact: Users are less likely to miss an update because assets appeared a little later than the release itself.

releases.title: Fork identity and upstream visibility
releases.summary: This build made the relationship between the fork and the original project much clearer.
highlights: About started showing fork version, upstream version, and upstream commit.
Upstream sync automation groundwork was added for cleaner future maintenance.
userImpact: Users can see exactly which official base this fork was built from.

releases.title: Standalone fork packaging
releases.summary: This build introduced the independent v2rayNG-Pro app identity.
highlights: The Android package name was separated so the fork installs alongside official v2rayNG.
Update checks and release assets were redirected to the fork repository.
userImpact: The fork no longer replaces the official app on the same device.
